CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, value VARCHAR);
SELECT starts_with(value, 'a') FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT starts_with(value, 'x') FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (2, 5);
SELECT id, value, starts_with(value, 'ab') AS starts_ab FROM test_strings;
SELECT ts.id, ts.value, ps.prefix, starts_with(ts.value, ps.prefix) AS matches FROM test_strings ts JOIN prefix_strings ps ON starts_with(ts.value, ps.prefix);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE starts_with(value, '');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE starts_with(value, 'abc');
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE starts_with(value, (SELECT prefix FROM prefix_strings LIMIT 1));

SELECT ss.id, ss.value, starts_with(ss.value, 'a') AS starts_with_a, starts_with(ss.value, (SELECT prefix FROM prefix_strings WHERE prefix = 'xyz')) AS starts_with_xyz FROM test_strings ss WHERE starts_with(ss.value, 'a') OR starts_with(ss.value, 'xyz');
SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE starts_with(value, (SELECT prefix FROM prefix_strings WHERE prefix = 'def')) AND value != 'def';
